[[File:YobitoTorii.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Torii|Torii]] as seen from the approach]] [[File:Yashiro.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Hashira.jpg|thumb|Pillars labeled as {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja|相馬中村神社跡地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''{{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}''' (よびとじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Yobito, [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]].
== Overview == Yobito Shrine was founded and has been maintained by the early settlers of this area, continuing to the present day. In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within its precincts.
== History == In 1914, due to the subdivision release of {{ill|Tanaka Ranch|ja|田中牧場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Ranch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the number of entry points into Yobito gradually increased. The origins of Yobito Shrine begin with {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who, as the initiator, held discussions with the then-settlers regarding the establishment of a shrine. Receiving a donation of a shrine site measuring 2 tan, 6 noh, and 24 bo at Yobito 267 from {{ill|Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja|田中清輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Kiyosuke|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in December 1915, Shimada Shōtarō was appointed as the head parishioner representative, and a main hall (measuring 4 shaku wide and 3 shaku deep) along with one torii were built. The head priest of {{ill|Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja|川向三吉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Abashiri Town received a branch spirit of both {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇大神宮}} and {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, and consecrated this land as sacred.
In July 1916, through the voluntary service of a youth group, the approach pathway was renovated and a new large torii was erected along the national highway. Since then, festivals and shrine events have been carried out by the youth group.
In September 1929 (Shōwa 4), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} proposed the construction of a new shrine building. After discussions with all parishioners and securing the support of 150 parishioners, and by soliciting donations for construction costs from local residents (with Shimada Shōtarō’s 150 yen leading a total of 2,360 yen in contributions), the shrine was built by Yobito’s master carpenter {{ill|Saitō Saijirō|ja|斎藤才次郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Saijirō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, many shrine fixtures were also donated.
* One set of mirror stand with sacred mirror * One sacred mirror for true sakura * One set of sacred bells * Two sets of five-colored flags * One set of sword-hilt bells * One curtain for the haiden * One drum * One curtain for the main hall * Eight sets of the “Three Treasures” * One pair of sacred lanterns * Two sets of sacred lantern stands
In 1958 (Shōwa 33), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja|久守席司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conceived the idea of renovating the severely decayed [[:en:Torii|torii]] and constructing new stone steps along the steep approach. After several meetings with involved parties, although the costs were very high, through the dedicated cooperation of {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and several others and receiving substantial donations, a large concrete torii was erected, and in the following year, 1959 (Shōwa 34), 123 stone steps were completed along the main approach. The contract was executed by the Noguchi Stone Materials Shop in Bihoro, with construction costs of 40,000 yen for the torii and 240,000 yen for the stone steps. In addition, the main hall, haiden, tin-roofed entertainment hall, notice board for the hand-washing area, and electrical wiring were installed. The total donations reached 439,500 yen.
Since its establishment, the shrine properties (land and buildings purchased as shrine assets) were registered under the names of successive head representatives or district chiefs. However, due to the frequent need to change registration whenever these co-owners relocated or died, the registration procedures became very cumbersome. To resolve this, the establishment of a shrine corporation was proposed by {{ill|Atsushi Nakazawa|ja|中沢敦司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsushi Nakazawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in October 1960 the corporate organization was approved, and all assets were transferred to the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}.
In 1967, as part of the 50th anniversary project of the establishment of Yobito Shrine, under the initiative of the head representative {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the miscellaneous trees within the shrine precincts were sold, and the funds were used to repair the main hall and haiden and to reline them entirely with tin. The project was completed with sales of 137,000 yen.
In April 1995 (Heisei 7), {{ill|Kagami Kunio|ja|加々見国男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Kunio|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tode Masakazu|ja|戸出正一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tode Masakazu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were presented with letters of appreciation in recognition of their long-term contributions to strengthening the shrine's management.
In May 1998 (Heisei 10), renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainting of the haiden roof were carried out, and a new storehouse was constructed.
In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. Also, 100 [[:en:Cherry blossom|Sakura]] trees were planted along the approach through the voluntary service of the youth group. * 1922 (Taishō 12) – Planted several dozen trees, including Todomatsu and Sakura, as a commemorative act. * September 1929 (Shōwa 4) – Constructed a new shrine building. * 1939 (Shōwa 14) – Donated a sacred offering box. * 1954 (Shōwa 29) – Constructed a kaguraden through parishioner service. * 1958 (Shōwa 33) – Received a donation for a large concrete torii. * 1959 (Shōwa 34) – Completed 123 stone steps along the main approach and donated three pairs of large banners (each 3 ken wide). * 1960 (Shōwa 35) – Established the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}. * 1967 (Shōwa 42) – As part of the 50th anniversary project, sold miscellaneous trees on the shrine grounds to fund repairs and complete re-lining with tin (137,000 yen obtained). * March 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Planted 100 Sakura trees along the shrine precincts by the voluntary service of students from {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in commemoration of the 100th anniversary of the opening of the road. * August 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Donated two 30-shaku banners and one pair of 5-ken banners. * April 1994 (Heisei 6) – Planted 50 [[:en:Picea glehnii|Akaezomatsu]] trees donated by {{ill|Yusaku Yosai|ja|遊佐幸吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusaku Yosai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} through the voluntary service of shrine officials. * April 1995 (Heisei 7) – Presented letters of appreciation to Kagami Kunio and Tode Masakazu for their long-term contributions. * May 1998 (Heisei 10) – Carried out renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainted the haiden roof; a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Festival Days == Until 1921 (Taishō 11), the "Spring Festival Day" was April 20 and the "Autumn Festival Day" was September 20. In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
== Sōma Myōken Shrine == [[File:ChuoTenmonkyo.jpg|thumb|The torii of {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} as seen from the approach]] [[File:Chusendo.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Kikakusa.jpg|thumb|Pillars identified as ruins of {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}]] In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall of Yobito Shrine.

In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. Also, 100 [[:en:Cherry blossom|Sakura]] trees were planted along the approach through the voluntary service of the youth group. * 1922 (Taishō 12) – Planted several dozen trees, including Todomatsu and Sakura, as a commemorative act. * September 1929 (Shōwa 4) – Constructed a new shrine building. * 1939 (Shōwa 14) – Donated a sacred offering box. * 1954 (Shōwa 29) – Constructed a kaguraden through parishioner service. * 1958 (Shōwa 33) – Received a donation for a large concrete torii. * 1959 (Shōwa 34) – Completed 123 stone steps along the main approach and donated three pairs of large banners (each 3 ken wide). * 1960 (Shōwa 35) – Established the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}. * 1967 (Shōwa 42) – As part of the 50th anniversary project, sold miscellaneous trees on the shrine grounds to fund repairs and complete re-lining with tin (137,000 yen obtained). * March 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Planted 100 Sakura trees along the shrine precincts by the voluntary service of students from {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in commemoration of the 100th anniversary of the opening of the road. * August 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Donated two 30-shaku banners and one pair of 5-ken banners. * April 1994 (Heisei 6) – Planted 50 [[:en:Picea glehnii|Akaezomatsu]] trees donated by {{ill|Yusaku Yosai|ja|遊佐幸吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusaku Yosai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} through the voluntary service of shrine officials. * April 1995 (Heisei 7) – Presented letters of appreciation to Kagami Kunio and Tode Masakazu for their long-term contributions. * May 1998 (Heisei 10) – Carried out renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainted the haiden roof; a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Festival Days == Until 1921 (Taishō 11), the "Spring Festival Day" was April 20 and the "Autumn Festival Day" was September 20. In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
== Sōma Myōken Shrine == [[File:ChuoTenmonkyo.jpg|thumb|The torii of {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} as seen from the approach]] [[File:Chusendo.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Kikakusa.jpg|thumb|Pillars identified as ruins of {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}]] In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall of Yobito Shrine.
== Surrounding Facilities == * {{ill|JR Hokkaido|ja|JR北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道旅客鉄道|1=Hokkaido Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498930}} [[:en:Sekihoku Main Line|Ishikiri Main Line]] [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] * {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Tokyo University of Agriculture|Tokyo University of Agriculture]], Faculty of Bioscience == See Also == {{commonscat|Yobito-jinja}} * {{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史「ふるさと呼人」|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yobito-jinja]][[ja:呼人神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20899316]] Wikidata name: Yobito-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}} | image = File:Yobito.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]] Yobito 267 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|57|38.5|N|144|13|13.8|E|region:JP-01_scale:10000|display=inline,title|name=呼人神社}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} | type = | 創建 = December 1914 (Taishō 4) | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = April 20 and September 10 | 神事 = | 地図 = | location = {{ill|Abashiri|WD=Q305640}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.96069444444444|N|144.2205|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:YobitoTorii.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Torii|Torii]] as seen from the approach]] [[File:Yashiro.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Hashira.jpg|thumb|Pillars labeled as {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja|相馬中村神社跡地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''{{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}''' (よびとじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Yobito, [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]].
== Overview == Yobito Shrine was founded and has been maintained by the early settlers of this area, continuing to the present day. In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within its precincts.
== History == In 1914, due to the subdivision release of {{ill|Tanaka Ranch|ja|田中牧場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Ranch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the number of entry points into Yobito gradually increased. The origins of Yobito Shrine begin with {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who, as the initiator, held discussions with the then-settlers regarding the establishment of a shrine. Receiving a donation of a shrine site measuring 2 tan, 6 noh, and 24 bo at Yobito 267 from {{ill|Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja|田中清輔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in December 1915, Shimada Shōtarō was appointed as the head parishioner representative, and a main hall (measuring 4 shaku wide and 3 shaku deep) along with one torii were built. The head priest of {{ill|Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja|川向三吉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Abashiri Town received a branch spirit of both {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇大神宮}} and {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, and consecrated this land as sacred.
In July 1916, through the voluntary service of a youth group, the approach pathway was renovated and a new large torii was erected along the national highway. Since then, festivals and shrine events have been carried out by the youth group.
In September 1929 (Shōwa 4), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} proposed the construction of a new shrine building. After discussions with all parishioners and securing the support of 150 parishioners, and by soliciting donations for construction costs from local residents (with Shimada Shōtarō’s 150 yen leading a total of 2,360 yen in contributions), the shrine was built by Yobito’s master carpenter {{ill|Saitō Saijirō|ja|斎藤才次郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Saijirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, many shrine fixtures were also donated.
* One set of mirror stand with sacred mirror * One sacred mirror for true sakura * One set of sacred bells * Two sets of five-colored flags * One set of sword-hilt bells * One curtain for the haiden * One drum * One curtain for the main hall * Eight sets of the “Three Treasures” * One pair of sacred lanterns * Two sets of sacred lantern stands
In 1958 (Shōwa 33), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja|久守席司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conceived the idea of renovating the severely decayed [[:en:Torii|torii]] and constructing new stone steps along the steep approach. After several meetings with involved parties, although the costs were very high, through the dedicated cooperation of {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and several others and receiving substantial donations, a large concrete torii was erected, and in the following year, 1959 (Shōwa 34), 123 stone steps were completed along the main approach. The contract was executed by the Noguchi Stone Materials Shop in Bihoro, with construction costs of 40,000 yen for the torii and 240,000 yen for the stone steps. In addition, the main hall, haiden, tin-roofed entertainment hall, notice board for the hand-washing area, and electrical wiring were installed. The total donations reached 439,500 yen.
Since its establishment, the shrine properties (land and buildings purchased as shrine assets) were registered under the names of successive head representatives or district chiefs. However, due to the frequent need to change registration whenever these co-owners relocated or died, the registration procedures became very cumbersome. To resolve this, the establishment of a shrine corporation was proposed by {{ill|Atsushi Nakazawa|ja|中沢敦司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsushi Nakazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in October 1960 the corporate organization was approved, and all assets were transferred to the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}.
In 1967, as part of the 50th anniversary project of the establishment of Yobito Shrine, under the initiative of the head representative {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the miscellaneous trees within the shrine precincts were sold, and the funds were used to repair the main hall and haiden and to reline them entirely with tin. The project was completed with sales of 137,000 yen.
In April 1995 (Heisei 7), {{ill|Kagami Kunio|ja|加々見国男|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Kunio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tode Masakazu|ja|戸出正一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tode Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were presented with letters of appreciation in recognition of their long-term contributions to strengthening the shrine's management.
In May 1998 (Heisei 10), renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainting of the haiden roof were carried out, and a new storehouse was constructed.
In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Festival Days == Until 1921 (Taishō 11), the "Spring Festival Day" was April 20 and the "Autumn Festival Day" was September 20. In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.

The origins of Yobito Shrine begin with {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who, as the initiator, held discussions with the then-settlers regarding the establishment of a shrine. Receiving a donation of a shrine site measuring 2 tan, 6 noh, and 24 bo at Yobito 267 from {{ill|Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja|田中清輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in December 1915, Shimada Shōtarō was appointed as the head parishioner representative, and a main hall (measuring 4 shaku wide and 3 shaku deep) along with one torii were built. The head priest of {{ill|Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja|川向三吉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Abashiri Town received a branch spirit of both {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇大神宮}} and {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, and consecrated this land as sacred.
In July 1916, through the voluntary service of a youth group, the approach pathway was renovated and a new large torii was erected along the national highway. Since then, festivals and shrine events have been carried out by the youth group.
In September 1929 (Shōwa 4), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} proposed the construction of a new shrine building. After discussions with all parishioners and securing the support of 150 parishioners, and by soliciting donations for construction costs from local residents (with Shimada Shōtarō’s 150 yen leading a total of 2,360 yen in contributions), the shrine was built by Yobito’s master carpenter {{ill|Saitō Saijirō|ja|斎藤才次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Saijirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, many shrine fixtures were also donated.
* One set of mirror stand with sacred mirror * One sacred mirror for true sakura * One set of sacred bells * Two sets of five-colored flags * One set of sword-hilt bells * One curtain for the haiden * One drum * One curtain for the main hall * Eight sets of the “Three Treasures” * One pair of sacred lanterns * Two sets of sacred lantern stands
In 1958 (Shōwa 33), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja|久守席司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conceived the idea of renovating the severely decayed [[:en:Torii|torii]] and constructing new stone steps along the steep approach. After several meetings with involved parties, although the costs were very high, through the dedicated cooperation of {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and several others and receiving substantial donations, a large concrete torii was erected, and in the following year, 1959 (Shōwa 34), 123 stone steps were completed along the main approach. The contract was executed by the Noguchi Stone Materials Shop in Bihoro, with construction costs of 40,000 yen for the torii and 240,000 yen for the stone steps. In addition, the main hall, haiden, tin-roofed entertainment hall, notice board for the hand-washing area, and electrical wiring were installed. The total donations reached 439,500 yen.
Since its establishment, the shrine properties (land and buildings purchased as shrine assets) were registered under the names of successive head representatives or district chiefs. However, due to the frequent need to change registration whenever these co-owners relocated or died, the registration procedures became very cumbersome. To resolve this, the establishment of a shrine corporation was proposed by {{ill|Atsushi Nakazawa|ja|中沢敦司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsushi Nakazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in October 1960 the corporate organization was approved, and all assets were transferred to the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}.
In 1967, as part of the 50th anniversary project of the establishment of Yobito Shrine, under the initiative of the head representative {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the miscellaneous trees within the shrine precincts were sold, and the funds were used to repair the main hall and haiden and to reline them entirely with tin. The project was completed with sales of 137,000 yen.
In April 1995 (Heisei 7), {{ill|Kagami Kunio|ja|加々見国男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Kunio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tode Masakazu|ja|戸出正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tode Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were presented with letters of appreciation in recognition of their long-term contributions to strengthening the shrine's management.
In May 1998 (Heisei 10), renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainting of the haiden roof were carried out, and a new storehouse was constructed.
In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. Also, 100 [[:en:Cherry blossom|Sakura]] trees were planted along the approach through the voluntary service of the youth group. * 1922 (Taishō 12) – Planted several dozen trees, including Todomatsu and Sakura, as a commemorative act. * September 1929 (Shōwa 4) – Constructed a new shrine building. * 1939 (Shōwa 14) – Donated a sacred offering box. * 1954 (Shōwa 29) – Constructed a kaguraden through parishioner service. * 1958 (Shōwa 33) – Received a donation for a large concrete torii. * 1959 (Shōwa 34) – Completed 123 stone steps along the main approach and donated three pairs of large banners (each 3 ken wide). * 1960 (Shōwa 35) – Established the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}. * 1967 (Shōwa 42) – As part of the 50th anniversary project, sold miscellaneous trees on the shrine grounds to fund repairs and complete re-lining with tin (137,000 yen obtained). * March 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Planted 100 Sakura trees along the shrine precincts by the voluntary service of students from {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in commemoration of the 100th anniversary of the opening of the road. * August 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Donated two 30-shaku banners and one pair of 5-ken banners. * April 1994 (Heisei 6) – Planted 50 [[:en:Picea glehnii|Akaezomatsu]] trees donated by {{ill|Yusaku Yosai|ja|遊佐幸吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusaku Yosai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} through the voluntary service of shrine officials. * April 1995 (Heisei 7) – Presented letters of appreciation to Kagami Kunio and Tode Masakazu for their long-term contributions. * May 1998 (Heisei 10) – Carried out renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainted the haiden roof; a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Festival Days == Until 1921 (Taishō 11), the "Spring Festival Day" was April 20 and the "Autumn Festival Day" was September 20. In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
== Sōma Myōken Shrine == [[File:ChuoTenmonkyo.jpg|thumb|The torii of {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} as seen from the approach]] [[File:Chusendo.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Kikakusa.jpg|thumb|Pillars identified as ruins of {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}]] In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall of Yobito Shrine.
== Surrounding Facilities == * {{ill|JR Hokkaido|ja|JR北海道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道旅客鉄道|1=Hokkaido Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498930}} [[:en:Sekihoku Main Line|Ishikiri Main Line]] [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] * {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Tokyo University of Agriculture|Tokyo University of Agriculture]], Faculty of Bioscience == See Also == {{commonscat|Yobito-jinja}} * {{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史「ふるさと呼人」|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == * 『{{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史 ふるさと呼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, compiled by the Yobito Ward Committee, Abashiri City, 1999.

[[File:YobitoTorii.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Torii|Torii]] as seen from the approach]] [[File:Yashiro.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Hashira.jpg|thumb|Pillars labeled as {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja|相馬中村神社跡地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''{{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}''' (よびとじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Yobito, [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]].
== Overview == Yobito Shrine was founded and has been maintained by the early settlers of this area, continuing to the present day. In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within its precincts.
== History == In 1914, due to the subdivision release of {{ill|Tanaka Ranch|ja|田中牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Ranch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the number of entry points into Yobito gradually increased. The origins of Yobito Shrine begin with {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who, as the initiator, held discussions with the then-settlers regarding the establishment of a shrine. Receiving a donation of a shrine site measuring 2 tan, 6 noh, and 24 bo at Yobito 267 from {{ill|Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja|田中清輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in December 1915, Shimada Shōtarō was appointed as the head parishioner representative, and a main hall (measuring 4 shaku wide and 3 shaku deep) along with one torii were built. The head priest of {{ill|Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja|川向三吉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Abashiri Town received a branch spirit of both {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇大神宮}} and {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, and consecrated this land as sacred.
In July 1916, through the voluntary service of a youth group, the approach pathway was renovated and a new large torii was erected along the national highway. Since then, festivals and shrine events have been carried out by the youth group.
In September 1929 (Shōwa 4), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} proposed the construction of a new shrine building. After discussions with all parishioners and securing the support of 150 parishioners, and by soliciting donations for construction costs from local residents (with Shimada Shōtarō’s 150 yen leading a total of 2,360 yen in contributions), the shrine was built by Yobito’s master carpenter {{ill|Saitō Saijirō|ja|斎藤才次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Saijirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, many shrine fixtures were also donated.
* One set of mirror stand with sacred mirror * One sacred mirror for true sakura * One set of sacred bells * Two sets of five-colored flags * One set of sword-hilt bells * One curtain for the haiden * One drum * One curtain for the main hall * Eight sets of the “Three Treasures” * One pair of sacred lanterns * Two sets of sacred lantern stands
In 1958 (Shōwa 33), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja|久守席司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conceived the idea of renovating the severely decayed [[:en:Torii|torii]] and constructing new stone steps along the steep approach. After several meetings with involved parties, although the costs were very high, through the dedicated cooperation of {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and several others and receiving substantial donations, a large concrete torii was erected, and in the following year, 1959 (Shōwa 34), 123 stone steps were completed along the main approach. The contract was executed by the Noguchi Stone Materials Shop in Bihoro, with construction costs of 40,000 yen for the torii and 240,000 yen for the stone steps. In addition, the main hall, haiden, tin-roofed entertainment hall, notice board for the hand-washing area, and electrical wiring were installed. The total donations reached 439,500 yen.
Since its establishment, the shrine properties (land and buildings purchased as shrine assets) were registered under the names of successive head representatives or district chiefs. However, due to the frequent need to change registration whenever these co-owners relocated or died, the registration procedures became very cumbersome. To resolve this, the establishment of a shrine corporation was proposed by {{ill|Atsushi Nakazawa|ja|中沢敦司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsushi Nakazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in October 1960 the corporate organization was approved, and all assets were transferred to the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}.
In 1967, as part of the 50th anniversary project of the establishment of Yobito Shrine, under the initiative of the head representative {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the miscellaneous trees within the shrine precincts were sold, and the funds were used to repair the main hall and haiden and to reline them entirely with tin. The project was completed with sales of 137,000 yen.
In April 1995 (Heisei 7), {{ill|Kagami Kunio|ja|加々見国男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Kunio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tode Masakazu|ja|戸出正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tode Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were presented with letters of appreciation in recognition of their long-term contributions to strengthening the shrine's management.
In May 1998 (Heisei 10), renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainting of the haiden roof were carried out, and a new storehouse was constructed.
In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.

a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.

In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
== Sōma Myōken Shrine == [[File:ChuoTenmonkyo.jpg|thumb|The torii of {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} as seen from the approach]] [[File:Chusendo.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Kikakusa.jpg|thumb|Pillars identified as ruins of {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}]] In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall of Yobito Shrine.
== Surrounding Facilities == * {{ill|JR Hokkaido|ja|JR北海道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道旅客鉄道|1=Hokkaido Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498930}} [[:en:Sekihoku Main Line|Ishikiri Main Line]] [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] * {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Tokyo University of Agriculture|Tokyo University of Agriculture]], Faculty of Bioscience == See Also == {{commonscat|Yobito-jinja}} * {{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史「ふるさと呼人」|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == * 『{{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史 ふるさと呼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, compiled by the Yobito Ward Committee, Abashiri City, 1999.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yobito-jinja]][[ja:呼人神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20899316]] Wikidata name: Yobito-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}} | image = File:Yobito.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]] Yobito 267 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|57|38.5|N|144|13|13.8|E|region:JP-01_scale:10000|display=inline,title|name=呼人神社}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} | type = | 創建 = December 1914 (Taishō 4) | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = April 20 and September 10 | 神事 = | 地図 = | location = {{ill|Abashiri|WD=Q305640}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.96069444444444|N|144.2205|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:YobitoTorii.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Torii|Torii]] as seen from the approach]] [[File:Yashiro.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Hashira.jpg|thumb|Pillars labeled as {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja|相馬中村神社跡地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''{{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}''' (よびとじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Yobito, [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]].
== Overview == Yobito Shrine was founded and has been maintained by the early settlers of this area, continuing to the present day. In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within its precincts.
== History == In 1914, due to the subdivision release of {{ill|Tanaka Ranch|ja|田中牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Ranch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the number of entry points into Yobito gradually increased. The origins of Yobito Shrine begin with {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who, as the initiator, held discussions with the then-settlers regarding the establishment of a shrine. Receiving a donation of a shrine site measuring 2 tan, 6 noh, and 24 bo at Yobito 267 from {{ill|Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja|田中清輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in December 1915, Shimada Shōtarō was appointed as the head parishioner representative, and a main hall (measuring 4 shaku wide and 3 shaku deep) along with one torii were built. The head priest of {{ill|Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja|川向三吉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Abashiri Town received a branch spirit of both {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇大神宮}} and {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, and consecrated this land as sacred.
In July 1916, through the voluntary service of a youth group, the approach pathway was renovated and a new large torii was erected along the national highway. Since then, festivals and shrine events have been carried out by the youth group.
In September 1929 (Shōwa 4), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} proposed the construction of a new shrine building. After discussions with all parishioners and securing the support of 150 parishioners, and by soliciting donations for construction costs from local residents (with Shimada Shōtarō’s 150 yen leading a total of 2,360 yen in contributions), the shrine was built by Yobito’s master carpenter {{ill|Saitō Saijirō|ja|斎藤才次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Saijirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, many shrine fixtures were also donated.
* One set of mirror stand with sacred mirror * One sacred mirror for true sakura * One set of sacred bells * Two sets of five-colored flags * One set of sword-hilt bells * One curtain for the haiden * One drum * One curtain for the main hall * Eight sets of the “Three Treasures” * One pair of sacred lanterns * Two sets of sacred lantern stands
In 1958 (Shōwa 33), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja|久守席司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conceived the idea of renovating the severely decayed [[:en:Torii|torii]] and constructing new stone steps along the steep approach. After several meetings with involved parties, although the costs were very high, through the dedicated cooperation of {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and several others and receiving substantial donations, a large concrete torii was erected, and in the following year, 1959 (Shōwa 34), 123 stone steps were completed along the main approach. The contract was executed by the Noguchi Stone Materials Shop in Bihoro, with construction costs of 40,000 yen for the torii and 240,000 yen for the stone steps. In addition, the main hall, haiden, tin-roofed entertainment hall, notice board for the hand-washing area, and electrical wiring were installed. The total donations reached 439,500 yen.
Since its establishment, the shrine properties (land and buildings purchased as shrine assets) were registered under the names of successive head representatives or district chiefs. However, due to the frequent need to change registration whenever these co-owners relocated or died, the registration procedures became very cumbersome. To resolve this, the establishment of a shrine corporation was proposed by {{ill|Atsushi Nakazawa|ja|中沢敦司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsushi Nakazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in October 1960 the corporate organization was approved, and all assets were transferred to the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}.
In 1967, as part of the 50th anniversary project of the establishment of Yobito Shrine, under the initiative of the head representative {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the miscellaneous trees within the shrine precincts were sold, and the funds were used to repair the main hall and haiden and to reline them entirely with tin. The project was completed with sales of 137,000 yen.
In April 1995 (Heisei 7), {{ill|Kagami Kunio|ja|加々見国男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Kunio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tode Masakazu|ja|戸出正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tode Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were presented with letters of appreciation in recognition of their long-term contributions to strengthening the shrine's management.
In May 1998 (Heisei 10), renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainting of the haiden roof were carried out, and a new storehouse was constructed.
In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. Also, 100 [[:en:Cherry blossom|Sakura]] trees were planted along the approach through the voluntary service of the youth group. * 1922 (Taishō 12) – Planted several dozen trees, including Todomatsu and Sakura, as a commemorative act. * September 1929 (Shōwa 4) – Constructed a new shrine building. * 1939 (Shōwa 14) – Donated a sacred offering box. * 1954 (Shōwa 29) – Constructed a kaguraden through parishioner service. * 1958 (Shōwa 33) – Received a donation for a large concrete torii. * 1959 (Shōwa 34) – Completed 123 stone steps along the main approach and donated three pairs of large banners (each 3 ken wide). * 1960 (Shōwa 35) – Established the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}. * 1967 (Shōwa 42) – As part of the 50th anniversary project, sold miscellaneous trees on the shrine grounds to fund repairs and complete re-lining with tin (137,000 yen obtained). * March 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Planted 100 Sakura trees along the shrine precincts by the voluntary service of students from {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in commemoration of the 100th anniversary of the opening of the road. * August 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Donated two 30-shaku banners and one pair of 5-ken banners. * April 1994 (Heisei 6) – Planted 50 [[:en:Picea glehnii|Akaezomatsu]] trees donated by {{ill|Yusaku Yosai|ja|遊佐幸吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusaku Yosai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} through the voluntary service of shrine officials. * April 1995 (Heisei 7) – Presented letters of appreciation to Kagami Kunio and Tode Masakazu for their long-term contributions. * May 1998 (Heisei 10) – Carried out renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainted the haiden roof; a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Festival Days == Until 1921 (Taishō 11), the "Spring Festival Day" was April 20 and the "Autumn Festival Day" was September 20. In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
== Sōma Myōken Shrine == [[File:ChuoTenmonkyo.jpg|thumb|The torii of {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} as seen from the approach]] [[File:Chusendo.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Kikakusa.jpg|thumb|Pillars identified as ruins of {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}]] In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall of Yobito Shrine.
== Surrounding Facilities == * {{ill|JR Hokkaido|ja|JR北海道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北海道旅客鉄道|1=Hokkaido Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498930}} [[:en:Sekihoku Main Line|Ishikiri Main Line]] [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] * {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Tokyo University of Agriculture|Tokyo University of Agriculture]], Faculty of Bioscience == See Also == {{commonscat|Yobito-jinja}} * {{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史「ふるさと呼人」|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == * 『{{ill|Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja|呼人百年史 ふるさと呼人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Hyakunenshiki “Furusato Yobito”|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, compiled by the Yobito Ward Committee, Abashiri City, 1999.

== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. Also, 100 [[:en:Cherry blossom|Sakura]] trees were planted along the approach through the voluntary service of the youth group. * 1922 (Taishō 12) – Planted several dozen trees, including Todomatsu and Sakura, as a commemorative act. * September 1929 (Shōwa 4) – Constructed a new shrine building. * 1939 (Shōwa 14) – Donated a sacred offering box. * 1954 (Shōwa 29) – Constructed a kaguraden through parishioner service. * 1958 (Shōwa 33) – Received a donation for a large concrete torii. * 1959 (Shōwa 34) – Completed 123 stone steps along the main approach and donated three pairs of large banners (each 3 ken wide). * 1960 (Shōwa 35) – Established the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}. * 1967 (Shōwa 42) – As part of the 50th anniversary project, sold miscellaneous trees on the shrine grounds to fund repairs and complete re-lining with tin (137,000 yen obtained). * March 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Planted 100 Sakura trees along the shrine precincts by the voluntary service of students from {{ill|Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja|網走市立呼人中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Municipal Yobito Middle School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in commemoration of the 100th anniversary of the opening of the road. * August 1968 (Shōwa 43) – Donated two 30-shaku banners and one pair of 5-ken banners. * April 1994 (Heisei 6) – Planted 50 [[:en:Picea glehnii|Akaezomatsu]] trees donated by {{ill|Yusaku Yosai|ja|遊佐幸吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusaku Yosai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} through the voluntary service of shrine officials. * April 1995 (Heisei 7) – Presented letters of appreciation to Kagami Kunio and Tode Masakazu for their long-term contributions. * May 1998 (Heisei 10) – Carried out renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainted the haiden roof; a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Festival Days == Until 1921 (Taishō 11), the "Spring Festival Day" was April 20 and the "Autumn Festival Day" was September 20. In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
== Sōma Myōken Shrine == [[File:ChuoTenmonkyo.jpg|thumb|The torii of {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} as seen from the approach]] [[File:Chusendo.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Kikakusa.jpg|thumb|Pillars identified as ruins of {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}]] In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall of Yobito Shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yobito-jinja]][[ja:呼人神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20899316]] Wikidata name: Yobito-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}} | image = File:Yobito.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]] Yobito 267 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|57|38.5|N|144|13|13.8|E|region:JP-01_scale:10000|display=inline,title|name=呼人神社}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} | type = | 創建 = December 1914 (Taishō 4) | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | festival = April 20 and September 10 | 神事 = | 地図 = | location = {{ill|Abashiri|WD=Q305640}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.96069444444444|N|144.2205|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:YobitoTorii.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Torii|Torii]] as seen from the approach]] [[File:Yashiro.jpg|thumb|The shrine building located behind the main hall]] [[File:Hashira.jpg|thumb|Pillars labeled as {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja|相馬中村神社跡地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''{{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}''' (よびとじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Yobito, [[:en:Abashiri|Abashiri City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]].
== Overview == Yobito Shrine was founded and has been maintained by the early settlers of this area, continuing to the present day. In 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within its precincts.
== History == In 1914, due to the subdivision release of {{ill|Tanaka Ranch|ja|田中牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Ranch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the number of entry points into Yobito gradually increased. The origins of Yobito Shrine begin with {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who, as the initiator, held discussions with the then-settlers regarding the establishment of a shrine. Receiving a donation of a shrine site measuring 2 tan, 6 noh, and 24 bo at Yobito 267 from {{ill|Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja|田中清輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Kiyosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in December 1915, Shimada Shōtarō was appointed as the head parishioner representative, and a main hall (measuring 4 shaku wide and 3 shaku deep) along with one torii were built. The head priest of {{ill|Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja|川向三吉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamukai Sankyō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Abashiri Town received a branch spirit of both {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇大神宮}} and {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, and consecrated this land as sacred.
In July 1916, through the voluntary service of a youth group, the approach pathway was renovated and a new large torii was erected along the national highway. Since then, festivals and shrine events have been carried out by the youth group.
In September 1929 (Shōwa 4), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} proposed the construction of a new shrine building. After discussions with all parishioners and securing the support of 150 parishioners, and by soliciting donations for construction costs from local residents (with Shimada Shōtarō’s 150 yen leading a total of 2,360 yen in contributions), the shrine was built by Yobito’s master carpenter {{ill|Saitō Saijirō|ja|斎藤才次郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Saijirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At that time, many shrine fixtures were also donated.
* One set of mirror stand with sacred mirror * One sacred mirror for true sakura * One set of sacred bells * Two sets of five-colored flags * One set of sword-hilt bells * One curtain for the haiden * One drum * One curtain for the main hall * Eight sets of the “Three Treasures” * One pair of sacred lanterns * Two sets of sacred lantern stands
In 1958 (Shōwa 33), the head parishioner representative {{ill|Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja|久守席司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Kumori Sekishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conceived the idea of renovating the severely decayed [[:en:Torii|torii]] and constructing new stone steps along the steep approach. After several meetings with involved parties, although the costs were very high, through the dedicated cooperation of {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and several others and receiving substantial donations, a large concrete torii was erected, and in the following year, 1959 (Shōwa 34), 123 stone steps were completed along the main approach. The contract was executed by the Noguchi Stone Materials Shop in Bihoro, with construction costs of 40,000 yen for the torii and 240,000 yen for the stone steps. In addition, the main hall, haiden, tin-roofed entertainment hall, notice board for the hand-washing area, and electrical wiring were installed. The total donations reached 439,500 yen.
Since its establishment, the shrine properties (land and buildings purchased as shrine assets) were registered under the names of successive head representatives or district chiefs. However, due to the frequent need to change registration whenever these co-owners relocated or died, the registration procedures became very cumbersome. To resolve this, the establishment of a shrine corporation was proposed by {{ill|Atsushi Nakazawa|ja|中沢敦司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsushi Nakazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in October 1960 the corporate organization was approved, and all assets were transferred to the shrine corporation {{ill|Yobito Shrine|ja|呼人神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yobito Shrine|qq=}}.
In 1967, as part of the 50th anniversary project of the establishment of Yobito Shrine, under the initiative of the head representative {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the miscellaneous trees within the shrine precincts were sold, and the funds were used to repair the main hall and haiden and to reline them entirely with tin. The project was completed with sales of 137,000 yen.
In April 1995 (Heisei 7), {{ill|Kagami Kunio|ja|加々見国男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami Kunio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tode Masakazu|ja|戸出正一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tode Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were presented with letters of appreciation in recognition of their long-term contributions to strengthening the shrine's management.
In May 1998 (Heisei 10), renovation work on the main hall’s foundation and repainting of the haiden roof were carried out, and a new storehouse was constructed.
In August 1998 (Heisei 10), {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} was enshrined within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.
== Chronology == * December 20, 1914 (Taishō 4) – Appointed {{ill|Shimada Shōtarō|ja|嶋田庄太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Shōtarō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as head parishioner representative and built one main hall (4 shaku wide, 3 shaku deep) and one torii. * July 1916 (Taishō 6) – Renovated the approach pathway and erected a new large torii along the national highway through the voluntary service of a youth group. * 1919 (Taishō 9) – Planted 100 [[:en:Larix kaempferi|Larix kaadama]] trees and 200 [[:en:Abies sachalinensis|Todomatsu]] trees on the shrine grounds by the voluntary service of the youth group, based on a donation from {{ill|Nakano Eisō|ja|中野栄松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakano Eisō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1920 (Taishō 10) – Purchased 9 tan and 8 noh of shrine land collectively by the parishioners; registered the property under the names of {{ill|Shimada Sachikirō|ja|嶋田佐吉郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Sachikirō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Tamehisa Zenjō|ja|為久善貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamehisa Zenjō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in that same year appointed Tamehisa Zenjō as head parishioner representative to plan shrine improvements. a new storehouse was built. * August 1998 (Heisei 10) – Enshrined {{ill|Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja|相馬妙見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Myōken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 呼人神社|1=Yobito Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20899316}} within the main hall in a relocation ceremony.

In commemoration of the opening of [[:en:Yobito Station|Yobito Station]] on September 1, 1922 (Taishō 12), the Autumn Festival Day was changed to September 1 and 2. Subsequently, considering the frequent rainy conditions during the 210th day period, it was revised to September 20, and from 1952 (Shōwa 27) it was finally changed to September 10. Today, the festival is generally held on a Saturday or Sunday in early August.
